AP-SKILL-RELOAD.description: Same Skill invoked >1× in a session for the same purpose — wastes full SKILL.md reload (~6k tokens each).
AP-SKILL-RELOAD.fix: After the first Skill load, call sub-commands via Bash directly (make, python, etc.). Do NOT re-invoke Skill(make,...) or Skill(chat,...) within the same session.
AP-SKILL-RELOAD.multi_call_note: Skills in multi_call_allowed are orchestrators designed for chained phase invocations (e.g. bloop *plan then bloop *impl). These are NOT flagged as AP-SKILL-RELOAD.

AP-MAKE-BYPASS.description: Bash runs pytest/ruff/pylint/mypy directly — bypasses make targets.
AP-MAKE-BYPASS.fix: Always use make <target>. Never call test runners or linters directly. Output is captured to build/build.out by the Makefile.

AP-RAW-VENV.description: Bash calls .venv/bin/<tool> directly — bypasses make targets and project tooling.
AP-RAW-VENV.fix: Use make <target> — never reference .venv/bin directly. If no make target exists for the tool, add one to Makefile.prj rather than calling .venv/bin.

AP-MAKE-PIPE.description: Bash pipes make output (e.g. `make test 2>&1 | tail -30`) — violates the make skill rule. mkf already captures output to build/build.out.
AP-MAKE-PIPE.fix: For test feedback: use `make test-q` (pytest -q --tb=short) — built-in concise output, no piping needed. For other targets: run `make <target>` then `tail -n 30 build/build.out`. Or use `make <target> V=-vv` to see failures live. NEVER pipe make output.

AP-VIA-GREP.description: Grep/Glob used for symbol, import, or function lookup when via is enabled.
AP-VIA-GREP.fix: Use mcp__via__via_query for symbol/relationship lookups. Reserve grep for content search (strings in files), not symbol navigation.

AP-VIA-READ.description: Read used on a source file that is never subsequently edited in this session — likely symbol hunting rather than pre-edit inspection.
AP-VIA-READ.fix: Use mcp__via__via_query to locate symbols and trace relationships. Read is only needed when you intend to edit the file or need its full content for review.

AP-DUP-READ.description: Same file Read 3+ times in one session without the file changing between reads.
AP-DUP-READ.fix: Read a file once and keep the relevant excerpt in context. Use Edit/Write to change it; only re-Read if you need to verify the result of an edit.
